source: Numerics/Distributions/Univariate/Uncertainty Analysis/BootstrapAnalysis.cs @ a2c4dbf -- X11 SELF-COMPUTED smoke oracle. BootstrapAnalysis (X7) fits a Normal to the dataset by MLE, then Estimate(probabilities, alpha) returns the UncertaintyAnalysisResults mode/mean curve + percentile CI band over the 3 non-exceedance probabilities. The mode curve is the fitted-distribution inverse_cdf, so mode_curve at prob 0.5 == the Normal MLE mean 15900.3860971 (the deterministic BFGS optimizer result, no RNG) -- a cross-language numeric oracle at rel 1e-6. curve_length == 3 is the deterministic length invariant. The percentile CI band is seeded-bootstrap dependent (deterministic across C++/R/Python via the shared MersenneTwister); X12 tightens the CI band to an exact oracle against the real Numerics BootstrapAnalysis via the emitter driver it must add.
